options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
What must you accomplish to win the game?

Survival: Survive a number of days to win. Chose the amount in "Days to Survive" option.

Bosses (Any): Defeat any boss selected in "Boss Defeat Requirement" to win.

Bosses (All): Defeat all bosses selected in "Boss Defeat Requirement" to win.

options.name: days_to_survive
options.description:
Only applies for the survival goal. The number of days your character must survive for the survival goal.

Each day would last 8 real life minutes on default settings. Being a ghost pauses your timer. Regenerating the world or dying in Wilderness mode resets your timer.

options.name: required_bosses
options.description:
Only applies for boss goal types. Which boss(es) are required to be defeated to beat the game?

Make sure correct regions, seasons, and day phases are enabled for your goal boss to be in logic.

"Random" chooses a random boss. If bosses are chosen along with it, it'll choose a random boss within your selection.

Valid Bosses:
Deerclops - Winter
Moose/Goose - Spring
Bearger - Autumn
Ancient Guardian - Cave (Full)
Antlion - Summer
Dragonfly
Bee Queen
Klaus - Winter
Toadstool - Cave
Malbatross - Ocean
Crab King - Ocean
Frostjaw - Ocean
Eye Of Terror - Night
Retinazor - Night
Spazmatism - Night
Nightmare Werepig - Cave (Full)
Scrappy Werepig - Cave (Full)
Ancient Fuelweaver - Cave (Full), Night
Celestial Champion - Ocean (Full)

Example: ['Deerclops', 'Moose/Goose', 'Bearger']

options.name: crafting_mode
options.description:
Change the crafting behavior?

Vanilla: Crafting behavior is vanilla.

Journey: Once you craft an item once, you can craft it again freely.

Free Samples: Once you unlock a recipe, you can craft one for free.

Free-Build: Once you unlock a recipe, you can always craft it.

Locked Ingredients: You cannot craft items that use one of your missing items as an ingredient.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: boss_fill_items
options.description: Choose what type of items a boss check can grant. Requires Boss Locations to be enabled.

options.name: seasons
options.description:
Which seasons will be enabled in your world?

This is not automatic. If changed from default, seasons will need to be set manually in your world settings!

options.name: starting_season
options.description:
Which season do you start with in your world?

This is not automatic. If not autumn, starting season will need to be set manually in your world settings!

options.name: day_phases
options.description:
Which day phases will be enabled in your world?

This is not automatic. If changed from default, day phases will need to be set manually in your world settings!

Set to Night only if you plan to play on a Lights Out world.

options.name: season_flow
options.description:
How do seasons progress in your world?

Normal: Seasons progress as default. Logic attempts to prepare you for seasons in time, but not guaranteed. Seasonal checks may not have progression items.

Unlockable: Season-changing items are progression. Seasonal checks may have progression items. Can optionally play with long seasons.

Unlockable Shuffled: Same as unlockable, except seasons are logically shuffled within the spheres. Can optionally play with long seasons.

options.name: cave_regions
options.description:
How far into the cave will your items and checks be shuffled?

None: No cave. Choose this if you're making a no-cave world!

Auto: Choose minimum regions based on your goal. (Survival goal will default to None. For boss goals, check the tooltip for "Boss Defeat Requirement".)

Light: Shuffle caves without ruins and archive.

Full: Shuffle caves, ruins, and archive.

options.name: ocean_regions
options.description:
How far into the ocean will your items and checks be shuffled?

None: No ocean checks.

Auto: Choose minimum regions based on your goal. (Survival goal will default to None. For boss goals, check the tooltip for "Boss Defeat Requirement".)

Light: Shuffle ocean without moonstorm.

Full: Shuffle ocean and moonstorm. Requires either Boss Locations to be "all" or Celestial Champion as your goal.

options.name: creature_locations
options.description:
Are non-boss creatures checks, by killing or non-violent interactions?

None: Creatures are not checks. (Disabling will remove a lot of checks. Excess items will be moved into your start inventory.)

All: All creatures are checks.

Peaceful: Only creatures that have a peaceful interaction are checks. Killing still also grants the check.

options.name: boss_locations
options.description:
Are boss defeats checks, other than ones listed in your goal condition?

None: No boss checks other than ones on your goal path.

Easy: Only easier bosses. These can be defeated even if playing solo with default difficulty.

All: Includes raid bosses. These are intended for multiplayer sessions, but can still be soloed with enough damage bonuses or creative strategies.

options.name: cooking_locations
options.description:
Have checks for cooking different foods in the crock pot? Your checks and example recipes are in the in-game tracker.
The wiki is also a good resource: https://dontstarve.wiki.gg/wiki/Dishes

None: No checks from cooking. (This will remove a lot of checks. Excess items will be moved into your start inventory.)

Regular: Get checks for cooking in the crock pot, excluding Warly's exclusives. (Up to 70 checks)

Warly Enabled: All crock pot recipes are checks, including Warly's exclusives.
Logic will expect you to have the ability to use the Portable Crock Pot. (Up to 81 checks)

Veggie Only: All recipes except meat and Warly exclusives. (Up to 32 checks)

Meat Only: All recipes except veggie and Warly exclusives. (Up to 48 checks)

options.name: farming_locations
options.description: Have checks for harvesting giant crops?

options.name: shuffle_starting_recipes
options.description:
Turn your basic starting recipes into Archipelago items?

This may leave you vulnerable to darkness on your first night! If you're not okay with this, you may want to add {Torch: 1} to your start inventory.

Sphere 1 will also be small, making generation more restrictive when generating alone, and is prone to fail if "Creature Locations" are off!

options.name: shuffle_no_unlock_recipes
options.description: Turn crafting recipes from the Ancient Pseudoscience Station and Celestial Altars into Archipelago items?

options.name: chesspiece_sketch_items
options.description:
Include Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figure Sketches as items?

This bypasses the need to assemble the marble sculptures for Shadow Pieces, while preventing specific progression-locking scenarios.

options.name: seed_items
options.description: Turn farm plant seeds into Archipelago items? When enabled, generic seeds can only grow weeds.

options.name: junk_item_amount
options.description:
Number of junk (stat change) and trap items to add to the item pool

If set too high, junk will leave no space for regular items, and overflow them into your starting inventory.

options.name: trap_items
options.description: Chance percentage junk items can be regular traps. These do not change the season. If combined with Season Traps Items, the percentage is split.

options.name: season_trap_items
options.description:
Chance percentage junk items can be season-changing traps. These can only be seasons that are enabled. If combined with Traps Items, the percentage is split.

Season traps trigger a cooldown for player-controlled season changes. The length is configurable in the mod options (default 1 day).

options.name: extra_damage_against_bosses
options.description:
This adds "Extra Damage Against Bosses" buffs as Archipelago items. High is recommended if planning to take on raid bosses solo.

Each stack of this buff gives all damage sources a permanent +10% damage against easier bosses and +25% damage against tougher ones.

This is exponential. With 10 stacks, this turns into x2.6 and x9.3 damage multipiers respectively. Multipliers can be configured in the mod configuration in the game's menu.

This amount is separate from any prefilled through "Boss Fill Items" setting.

options.name: damage_bonuses
options.description:
This adds "Damage Bonus" buffs as Archipelago items.

Each stack of this buff gives the player a permanent +10% damage against all mobs. It only affects a player's direct damage.

This is exponential. With 10 stacks, this turns into a x2.6 multipier. Multiplier can be configured in the mod configuration in the game's menu.

This amount is separate from any prefilled through "Boss Fill Items" setting.

options.name: skill_level
options.description:
What skill level should be considered for randomizer logic?

Easy: Adds useful items in logic and avoids harder solutions.

Advanced: Expects the player to be familiar with game mechanics.

Expert: Expects the player survive in riskier conditions, have minimal items, and know advanced tricks.

options.name: lighting_logic
options.description: Make portable light sources progression and include in logic?

options.name: weapon_logic
options.description: Make weapons and armor progression and include in logic?

options.name: season_gear_logic
options.description: Make protective seasonal gear progression and include in logic?

options.name: base_making_logic
options.description: Make quality-of-life structures such as Chest, Ice Box, and flooring progression and include in logic?

options.name: backpack_logic
options.description: Make backpacks progression and include in logic?

options.name: healing_logic
options.description: Make healing items progression and include in logic?

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
